Special resolution (needs 75%) to enable Indian-Owned-and-Controlled-Company (IOCC) status — required because India's FDI rules bar foreign-funded entities from the inventory-led e-commerce model (marketplace model is open to 100% FDI; inventory-based is prohibited). Failed at 72.35% (foreign shareholders balked at the ownership/control change), so Instamart legally cannot adopt inventory-led QC yet; Blinkit can only because Eternal restructured to IOCC. NOT a referendum on the economics. Postal ballot closed 20 May 2026; FDI basis see S40

CORRECTION (real-data validation): Eternal CFO Akshant Goyal (Q2FY26 call) stated ~1ppt (~100bps) EBITDA accretion + ~300bps gross-margin lift over 4-6 quarters. The earlier 60bps figure was misattributed to a 'Swiggy CFO' — Swiggy never transitioned (IOCC vote failed), so this is Blinkit's OBSERVED benefit used as the proxy for Instamart's potential. Capturing it requires Instamart's entity to first become IOCC (an FDI/ownership prerequisite, see row 84/S40), not merely a margin decision
